closure_gate.reason: The current target-audit top coordinate and the auxiliary direct-top average are compatible as a comparison, but no source-side extraction-response map has been emitted; therefore the direct-top theorem bridge is closed as a no-go on the current corpus.
reopen_only_when: codomain metadata is explicit in the public status surfaces
a response kernel maps the current top coordinate into the direct-top codomain without using the direct-top central value as an input
the propagated interval overlaps or explains the auxiliary direct-top entry

closure_verdict.why_no_forward_bridge: The current OPH/D10/D11/quark corpus emits a top coordinate in the PDG cross-section codomain Q007TP4. It does not emit an extraction-response kernel, mass-scheme conversion, or uncertainty-propagation certificate from Q007TP4 into the auxiliary direct-measurement codomain Q007TP. Any such conversion would choose external experimental/MC response data.

current_target_audit_coordinate.pdg_description: t-Quark Pole Mass from Cross-Section Measurements

formal_nonidentifiability_witness.source_constraints_on_lambda: none emitted by the current corpus
formal_nonidentifiability_witness.why_not_a_theorem: Choosing lambda from the auxiliary Q007TP central value would use the direct-top row as an input. Choosing lambda=0 would relabel Q007TP4 as Q007TP. Both choices satisfy the current target-audit top data equally well because the extraction-response codomain is absent.

local_next_steps: Keep both Q007TP and Q007TP4 as compare-only target coordinates.
Do not launch more workers for this lane unless a source-side or QFT extraction-response map is introduced.
Open a new issue only for a concrete response-kernel artifact, not for further border sharpening.

worker_result_policy.reason: A new worker pass is not useful until a source-side extraction-response object exists.
